humanSummary.text: Initial audits found P1 natural-key upsert failures. The branch repaired external id, local scan, seed target, and nested seed-target paths, with DB-backed re-audit passing. Remaining index/test hardening items are recorded as follow-ups.

findings.title: Catalog index definition regression coverage
findings.description: Catalog tests currently assert index presence by name, but do not assert the full index definitions for key columns, uniqueness, expression indexes, and ordering.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit passed DB-backed tests and recommended strengthening index tests with pg_get_indexdef checks.

findings.impact: Future migration/schema drift could preserve index names while changing uniqueness, expression-index behavior, or ordering semantics.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Catalog index tests assert pg_get_indexdef output for key unique and ordered indexes.
Tests cover the coalesced seed-target origin_ref expression index and priority-desc ordering index.

proposedDagNode.title: Catalog index definition regression tests
proposedDagNode.summary: Strengthen catalog migration tests to assert exact Postgres index definitions, not only index names.
deliverables: DB-backed catalog test querying pg_get_indexdef for natural-key and priority indexes
Assertions for uniqueness, coalesce(origin_ref, ''), and priority desc ordering
acceptanceCriteria: Changing catalog index uniqueness, expression columns, or ordering causes a DB test failure.
The index definition test runs under the @itotori/db test suite.
auditFocus: Catalog index name test passing despite definition drift

findings.title: Caller-supplied nested seed target entry id coverage
findings.description: The repaired nested seed-target regression covers omitted child IDs. A smaller test should also cover the caller-supplied localScanEntryId path where the supplied ID equals the parent entry ID.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit confirmed the omitted-child-ID path is fixed and suggested an explicit caller-supplied entry-id regression.

findings.impact: A future refactor could regress supplied nested entry IDs while preserving the omitted-ID case that originally failed.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: DB tests cover nested seed targets whose supplied localScanEntryId equals the parent local scan entry ID.
The test proves repeated local-scan natural-key upserts preserve correct parent linkage.

proposedDagNode.title: Nested seed target supplied-entry-id regression
proposedDagNode.summary: Add DB coverage for nested seed targets with caller-supplied localScanEntryId matching the parent scan entry.
deliverables: Focused @itotori/db regression test for nested seed targets with caller-supplied parent localScanEntryId
Assertions that repeated local-scan natural-key upserts preserve the persisted parent-child linkage
acceptanceCriteria: Caller-supplied parent entry IDs and omitted parent entry IDs both keep nested seed targets linked to the persisted local scan entry.
auditFocus: Nested seed target linkage regression when caller supplies parent entry id
